mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment growth above 200k per month signals strong labor market demand, which drives corporate earnings optimism and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k growth, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production and rise in inflation expectations creates a stagflationary environment that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real estate financing (RSAFS) alongside rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates overleveraged demand, which triggers a correction in equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls > 5% and HOUST rises > 3% in 1 month
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal incorrectly predicts positive returns in > 40% of triggered cases, or the pair's correlation coefficient drops below -0.3 in rolling 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) above 3% annually signals central bank intervention, which increases bond yields and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% YoY, or the signal's IC falls below 0.04 in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment (UNRATE) and rise in wage growth (CPIAUCSL) indicates labor market overheating, which prompts monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the joint effect's p-value exceeds 0.15 in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annually signals weak aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profits and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 falls below 2% growth, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong supply-side dynamics, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 2% MoM and CPIAUCSL changes < 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal incorrectly predicts negative returns in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression tests.
